appendMonsterAbilities.name: Aetherial Gateway
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: Once per turn, the primordial wizard can open a portal between two zones it can see. All portals close if the primordial wizard takes damage.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Nightmare
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The target immediately makes a skill check controlled by the phobiarus. If it fails, the target takes 6 magic damage.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Blood Burrow
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: 3 physical damage, and the blood eel gains advantage on all attacks against the target until the target scores 7 PRW to remove the eel.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Blood Drain
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: See monster notes/rules text for damage and any additional requirements/effects.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Hypnosis
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The elder vampire controls one skill check on the target's next turn.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Dark Harvest
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: Whenever the reaper deals damage, the target's spirit is bound. If they die before the end of the reaper's next turn, they are raised as an undead servant. Targets raised by the reaper have 8 HP and retain their STR bonus but lose all other skill bonuses. Each of the reaper's undead servants can make one skill check on the reaper's turn.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Soul Snatcher
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: Whenever the hollow kills a mortal enemy, the enemy becomes a wraith.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Dead Legion
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: Whenever the knight shade deals damage, the target's spirit is bound. If they die before the end of the knight shade's next turn, they are raised as an undead servant. Targets raised by the knight shade have 3 HP and retain their STR bonus but lose all other skill bonuses. Each of the knight shade's undead servants can make one skill check on the knight shade's turn.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Create Wight
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: Once per turn, the fleshweaver can turn a corpse within its reach into a wight.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Draining Cold
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: 2 magic damage and the target has disadvantage on their next skill check.

appendMonsters.name: Primordial Wizard
appendMonsters.loreText: Humans were the first mortal beings to harness the arcane power of dragonblood, forever changing the cosmic balance. They began experimenting with magic before the gods ruled the heavens above, and unraveled the mysteries of the aether before death could ever claim them.

appendMonsters.name: Scalari Tyrant
appendMonsters.loreText: Scalari are bipedal dragons, born from human flesh and dragonblood. Though they lack wings, they take on many aspects of dragons, including their iron scales, ravenous fire, and terrifying wrath. Scalari make for powerful, domineering rulers, who command absolute devotion from every subject.

appendMonsters.name: Kaöxi Dreamwalker
appendMonsters.loreText: Moulded from chaos and quenched in the void, the Kaoxi toy with fate in the formless aether. Their mystical third eye peers deep into the realm of dreams, and their innate mastery of magic is legendary.

appendMonsters.name: Sylvani Sovereign

equipment.name: Verdant Cloak
equipment.rulesText: The Sylvani sovereign gains advantage on its next skill check upon successful defense.

appendMonsters.loreText: Children of Eden, the Sylvani are blessed by the moon's silver embrace. Slender and graceful, they step in stride with primordial beasts. Ancient Sylvani rule over fae courts in Eden, representing each of the zodiac beasts that shape the future.

appendMonsters.name: Ragori Bloodfiend
appendMonsters.loreText: Demigods of war, the Ragori live and die by the drumbeat of conquest. They feast upon the blood of their enemies, taking on the strengths of their favoured prey. Ragori who feed exclusively on one type of blood may transform into different creatures entirely, such as orcs, ogres, or vampires. Bloodfiends are those who hunt down the mightiest creatures in the land to drink their blood and absorb their power.

appendMonsters.name: Vanori Shadebringer

equipment.name: Shade Cloak
equipment.rulesText: The Vanori shadebringer gains advantage on its next skill check upon successful defense.

appendMonsters.loreText: Demigods of time, the Vanori feed on death and desolation. While some Vanori isolate themselves from society, others actively bring about catastrophes to harvest dark power. So-called shadebringers call upon the forces of pestilence and plague, spreading mass death among mortal populations.

appendMonsters.name: Homunculus Headhunter
appendMonsters.loreText: Artificial life created by mortal magi, homunculi are an affront to the gods. Their arcane minds can process information at unparalleled speeds, but their behaviour is limited by the imagination of their creators. Those that are designed to hunt down and retrieve people are known as headhunters, and are widely feared for their single-minded efficiency.

appendMonsters.name: Astari Seer
appendMonsters.loreText: Children of the stars, the Astari see far beyond the borders of reality.

appendMonsters.name: Legnai Crusader
appendMonsters.loreText: Demigods of honour, the Legnai seek out and destroy injustice. They fly into battle on gilded wings, smiting their foes with righteous fury.

appendMonsters.name: Orc Chieftain
appendMonsters.loreText: Orcs are commanded by the strongest and meanest among them. Though they are not the most cunning leaders, orc chieftains are often able to coerce beasts and lesser monsters into their service with brute force.

appendMonsters.name: Ogre
appendMonsters.loreText: Ogres are massive brutes with enormous strength and an appetite to match. They are descended from a Ragori clan that fed exclusively on trolls, who lost in intelligence what they gained in strength. Ogres are incredibly dangerous, but are very easy to confuse and deceive.
notes: The ogre has disadvantage on all WIS checks.

appendMonsters.name: Gobanni Runesmith
appendMonsters.loreText: Demigods of craft, the Gobanni instill their works with eternal beauty. They are masters of enchantment, inscribing runes of power into their every creation.

appendMonsters.name: Boreani Windspeaker
appendMonsters.loreText: Sculpted from clouds and bestowed with a voice of rushing wind, the Boreani dance across the seas and skies. Those who guide the coming storm are known as windspeakers, singing the song of the tempest.

appendMonsters.name: Luminari Monk
appendMonsters.loreText: Radiant souls reincarnated by the sun's mercy, the Luminari bring light to dark places. Their golden gaze illuminates their surroundings, casting away corruption wherever they look. Many Luminari become wandering monks, who travel the pillar worlds in search of greater enlightenment.

appendMonsters.name: Undori Ironheart
appendMonsters.loreText: Forged from stone and fire, the Undori are guardians of the earth. Though their disposition is normally jovial, those who threaten the subterranean homes of the Undori may witness the rumbling inferno within. Those who are sworn to defend their underground domain are known as Ironhearts, and are staunch protectors.
notes: The Undori ironheart has extra HP equal to its STR bonus.

appendMonsters.name: Aiagi Shepherd

equipment.name: Harp
equipment.rulesText: The Aiagi shepherd gains advantage on its next skill check upon successful defense.

appendMonsters.loreText: Demigods of love, the Aiagi care for peaceful places. Their enchanting song soothes the spirit, and their hearts overflow with life.

appendMonsters.name: Livedi Troubadour

equipment.name: Jeering Mask
equipment.rulesText: The Livedi troubadour gains advantage on its next skill check upon successful defense.

appendMonsters.loreText: Demigods of trickery, the Livedi delight in causing drama. Many of them travel in nomadic bands, stirring up trouble wherever they go. They lure naive villagers in with music and performances, then loosen them up with drink. Once their guests are pliable, the Livedi coax out each visitor's secrets and then spread them throughout the village. By the time the community collapses, the troupe of Livedi is already on the road to the next town.

appendMonsters.name: Orc
appendMonsters.loreText: Orcs are brutish raiders who live for conquest and bloodshed. Orcs are descended from Ragori who fed exclusively on goblins, and often work closely with their fae cousins. Most orcs are dim-witted, but make up for their stupidity with strength and ferocity. Orcs multiply quickly when feasting on mortals, and are often used as infantry by evil leaders.

appendMonsters.name: Human Bandit
appendMonsters.loreText: Bandits are the lowest form of humanity, living only by the primal laws of might and fear. They rob, swindle, and kill any unarmed traveler unfortunate enough to cross their path, but often flee the moment a capable opponent arrives.

appendMonsters.name: Demon
appendMonsters.loreText: Beings of pure cruelty, demons feed on mortal suffering.

appendMonsters.name: Phobiarus
appendMonsters.loreText: Phobiari are embodiments of fear, terrorizing mortals with visions of agony and loss.

appendMonsters.name: Behemoth
appendMonsters.loreText: Enormous terrestrial monstrosities, behemoths charge through the hells leaving destruction in their wake. They have many eyes, many mouths, many horns, and many legs, striking wildly in every direction.

appendMonsters.name: Megurus
appendMonsters.loreText: Murderous spawn of the gods of war, meguri know nothing but bloodshed. Their jagged teeth run all the way down their torso, their mouths unfurling to devour their enemies whole. Grasping hands reach from their gaping maws, dragging nearby mortals in.

appendMonsters.name: Vizirus

equipment.name: Tattered Cloak
equipment.rulesText: The viziri gains advantage on its next skill check upon successful defense.

appendMonsters.loreText: Masked monsters who appear in mirrors, the viziri latch on to those whose secrecy harms others. They wear cloaks of tattered parchment, which conceal their countless crooked arms.

appendMonsters.name: Devil

equipment.name: Cursed Fiddle
equipment.rulesText: The devil gains advantage on its next skill check upon successful defense.

appendMonsters.loreText: Devils are malevolent tricksters who tempt mortals with crooked deals and costly pleasures. Created by the gods of trickery, devils covet mortal souls above all else.

appendMonsters.name: Gularus
appendMonsters.loreText: Bloated masses of pockmarked flesh, gulari are embodiments of gluttony. They consume any living thing they can get their grubby hands on, as well as magical energy.

appendMonsters.name: Balphora
appendMonsters.loreText: Goat-headed demons, balphora are the footsoldiers of the infernal legions. They are reincarnations of wicked mortals, and love to cause pain. They wield lashes dripping in agonizing venom, torturing those they strike from the inside out.

appendMonsters.name: Furus
appendMonsters.loreText: Embodiments of wrath, furi are savage warriors whose veins run with burning magma. They lash out at everything around them, driven to madness by their eternal rage.

appendMonsters.name: Invirus
appendMonsters.loreText: Vindictive monsters with long, reaching arms, inviri are embodiments of envy. They feed on emotional attachment, and steal items of sentimental value. Their grasping claws drain the life and spiritual fortitude from their victims, transferring it to the Invirus.

appendMonsters.name: Superus
appendMonsters.loreText: Arrogant and disdainful monsters, superi are embodiments of pride. While their true form is small and weak, they adopt a resplendent disguise to intimidate their victims. Superi feed on feelings of inferiority, and demand submission from mortals. Though they appear to be extremely dangerous, mortals who stand up to superi may find them to be less of a threat than they initially seem.
notes: The superus can only use its shapeshifting trait while at full HP.

appendMonsters.name: Hellhound
appendMonsters.loreText: Monstrous beasts whose snapping maws burn with hellfire, hellhounds serve as guardians and trackers for more powerful infernals.

appendMonsters.name: Blood Eel
appendMonsters.loreText: Horrific slithering monstrosities, blood eels are many people's worst nightmare. Like enormous leeches, they have concentric rings of jagged teeth and an instinctual thirst for blood. They latch onto their prey, burrowing into their flesh to lay eggs.

appendMonsters.name: Avarus
appendMonsters.loreText: Embodiments of greed, avari are jeering monsters that plague those who withhold their wealth from the needy. Their whispered mockery is known to drive their victims to madness.

appendMonsters.name: Imp
appendMonsters.loreText: Imps are damned souls reincarnated as devious winged monsters. Though they are a nuisance on their own, Imps often gather in large swarms that can strip all the flesh from a person's bones in a matter of minutes. Imps emit an ear-splitting sonic screech to attract more of their kind and disorient their prey. The screech of a single imp is painful, but the collective screeching of an entire swarm can kill.

appendMonsters.name: Elder Vampire

equipment.name: Shadow Cloak
equipment.rulesText: The elder vampire gains advantage on its next skill check upon successful defense.

appendMonsters.loreText: Over the course of centuries spent feeding upon mortals, vampires grow and master their dark powers. Elder vampires reign over empires of shadow, commanding the very night itself.
notes: The elder vampire takes 6 magic damage if it ends its turn in direct sunlight.
Instead of restoring HP, healing effects deal magic damage to the elder vampire equal to the amount of HP that would have been restored.
Blood Drain (STL vs. INS; range reach): 4 physical damage and the target loses a skill check on its next turn.

appendMonsters.name: Reaper
appendMonsters.loreText: Skeletal masters of necromancy, reapers feast on the souls of the living and raise armies of the dead.

appendMonsters.name: Vampire Lord
appendMonsters.loreText: Vampires follow a hierarchy based on age and dark power. Vampire lords are those who have infected numerous others with their dark curse, and spent decades developing their bond with darkness.
notes: The vampire lord takes 6 magic damage if it ends its turn in direct sunlight.
Instead of restoring HP, healing effects deal magic damage to the vampire lord equal to the amount of HP that would have been restored.
Blood Drain (STL vs. INS; range reach; only against an enemy within reach that it has advantage against): 4 physical damage and the target loses a skill check on its next turn.

appendMonsters.name: Mummy
appendMonsters.loreText: Preserved remains of ancient rulers, mummies awaken to curse those who would desecrate their tombs. Their raspy incantation is the last thing many grave robbers hear.

appendMonsters.name: Hollow
appendMonsters.loreText: Those who have their souls stolen by dark magic may become hollows. Their bodies become twisted and their faces are stretched into horrific masks. Hollows have an eternal hunger for mortal life, but they can never fill the void within.

appendMonsters.name: Knight Shade
appendMonsters.loreText: Noble warriors brought back to fight once more, knight shades lead legions of undead monsters.

appendMonsters.name: Fleshweaver
appendMonsters.loreText: Four-armed Gobanni resurrected by dark magic, fleshweavers excel at creating new undead.

appendMonsters.name: Vampire
appendMonsters.loreText: Bloodthirsty shadows, vampires cheat death by draining mortals of their vitality. It is said that the first vampires were born when the bloodthirsty Ragori, demi-gods of war, fed on the ill-omened Vanori, demi-gods of time. The combined powers of these two accursed bloodlines fused to create one of the most dreaded monsters to ever stalk the night. Vampirism is contagious, and those who survive a vampire bite inherit the curse. Not every mortal bloodline is equally susceptible to vampirism, and some can stave off its effects after a bout of illness. Luminari are particularly resistant to vampirism due to their close connection to the sun. On the opposite end of the spectrum, Ragori and Vanori are both especially vulnerable to vampirism, and can become especially powerful if they embrace their dark gifts.
notes: The vampire takes 6 magic damage if it ends its turn in direct sunlight.
Instead of restoring HP, healing effects deal magic damage to the vampire equal to the amount of HP that would have been restored.
Blood Drain (STL vs. INS; range reach; only against an enemy within reach that it has advantage against): 2 physical damage and the target loses a skill check on its next turn.

appendMonsters.name: Wraith
appendMonsters.loreText: Tormented souls stolen from the afterlife, wraiths radiate shadowy malice.
notes: Instead of restoring HP, healing effects deal magic damage to the wraith equal to the amount of HP that would have been restored.

appendMonsters.name: Wight
appendMonsters.loreText: Born of dark bloodletting rituals, wights are created by necromancers to serve as tireless warriors. They are not driven by the same mindless hunger as ghouls, and are easier for their dark masters to control. Their unholy strikes tear at their enemies' spirits, sapping their mana.

appendMonsters.name: Banshee
appendMonsters.loreText: Vengeful spirits, banshees emit a piercing wail that drains the body and weakens the soul.
notes: Instead of restoring HP, healing effects deal magic damage to the banshee equal to the amount of HP that would have been restored.

appendMonsters.name: Skeleton
appendMonsters.loreText: Unburdened bone imbued with bound souls, undead skeletons are quick and agile warriors.

appendMonsters.name: Ghost
appendMonsters.loreText: Lost souls with unfinished business, ghosts haunt the sites of their greatest suffering.
notes: Instead of restoring HP, healing effects deal magic damage to the ghost equal to the amount of HP that would have been restored.

appendMonsters.name: Scuttler
appendMonsters.loreText: Sinewy corpses that crawl with unnerving speed, scuttlers attack mortals in swarms.

appendMonsters.name: Ghoul
appendMonsters.loreText: Ghouls are twisted corpses given false life by corrupt magic. They have an insatiable hunger for mortal flesh, and mindlessly pursue the living.
